description: Take in the genomic coordinates of variants to map how ref/alt sequence changes impact the function of TF binding sites. In biomedical applications, comparisons can be made between reference and alternate alleles that are associated with diseases or changes in gene expression.
author: GenePattern Team
plan:
## tfsites.CompareAcrossSeqsFromGenomicVariants GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
tfsites.CompareAcrossSeqsFromGenomicVariants is a GenePattern module that Take in the genomic coordinates of variants to map how ref/alt sequence changes impact the function of TF binding sites. In biomedical applications, comparisons can be made between reference and alternate alleles that are associated with diseases or changes in gene expression.

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 16 parameters:
- Required: genome.file, variant.file, pos.index.type, window.size, analysis.name, minimum.binding.change
- Optional: tf.affinity.information, tf.affinity.files, minimum.pwm.score, pwm.input, motif.input.file, motif.input.format, motif.filter.keywords, motif.use.pseudocounts, motif.background.frequencies, motif.output.name

### 3. Command Line
```
python /build/tfsites-webportal/06-compareTfSitesAcrossSequencesFromGenomicVariants/06-combined-generateMotifDatabase-and-compareTfSitesAcrossSequencesFromGenomicVariants.py -g <genome.file> -v <variant.file> -w <window.size>  <tf.affinity.information> <tf.affinity.files>  <pwm.input> -m <minimum.binding.change> -s <minimum.pwm.score> -t <pos.index.type> -n <analysis.name> <motif.input.file> <motif.input.format> <motif.filter.keywords> <motif.use.pseudocounts> <motif.background.frequencies>  <motif.output.name> -d .
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: genepattern/tfsites\:17.0.16

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: genome.file
parameters.description: Pickled genome file that corresponds to the genomic coordinates provided. This is used to extract the sequences to be compared. Some genomes are available on the TFSites hosted data S3 bucket.

parameters.name: variant.file
parameters.description: Tab-separated file containing the list of genomic coordinates to analyze. There should be at least 5 columns in this file, including the chromosome, position, reference allele, alternate + allele, and hypothesis (values can include gof/lof/both/na).

parameters.name: pos.index.type
parameters.description: Specify whether position is zero or one indexed.

parameters.name: window.size
parameters.description: Length of the binding sites that are being analyzed. This will be used to determine the number of nucleotides to include on each side of a variant when extracting the surrounding sequence.

parameters.name: tf.affinity.information
parameters.description: File containing all the information for the transcription factors being analyzed, including its name, binding site definition, desired color on the plot, any PBM relative affinity data, and any PFM relative score data.

parameters.name: tf.affinity.files
parameters.description: TF affinity files referenced in the tf affinity information

parameters.name: analysis.name
parameters.description: Name of the analysis. Used as the prefix of all output filenames.

parameters.name: minimum.pwm.score
parameters.description: The minimum PWM binding score to predict a site

parameters.name: pwm.input
parameters.description: JASPAR formatted file with multiple PWMs. You can generate a PWM with the tfsites.GenerateMotifDatabase module.

parameters.name: minimum.binding.change
parameters.description: The minimum change of affinity or PWM binding score classify as "increase" or "decrease" in score or affinity.

parameters.name: motif.input.file
parameters.description: File containing matrix data for motif database generation (JASPAR format). If provided, motif database will be generated first.

parameters.name: motif.input.format
parameters.description: Specify whether input file contains PFMs or PWMs. Required if motif input file is provided.

parameters.name: motif.filter.keywords
parameters.description: File containing keywords to filter motifs during database generation.

parameters.name: motif.use.pseudocounts
parameters.description: Whether to use pseudocounts when generating motif database.

parameters.name: motif.background.frequencies
parameters.description: Background frequency for each nucleotide (comma-separated string in order of ATCG).

parameters.name: motif.output.name
parameters.description: Name of motif database output file. If not specified, temporary file will be created.
